content: The linguistic definition of "reflexion" as both optical reflection and deliberate metacognitive reflection can ground a redesign of LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where LLM self-reflection is framed as a sequential process of first optically reflecting off the environment (i.e., reflecting task feedback and external context) before engaging in deliberate metacognitive reflexion to revise outputs. This hybrid framework leverages the dual lexical meaning of reflexion to address a common limitation of current LLM self-reflection that overprioritizes internal metacognition over grounding in external feedback.
